metadata.description: Sayr (melodic path) definitions for all maqamat

zone_definitions.description: Standard zone types used across all maqamat

lower.description: Below tonic, used for emphasis and depth

middle.description: Lower jins upper portion, approaching ghammaz

saba.special_notes: Saba has a unique 'sighing' quality - the lowered 4th degree creates plaintive feeling

sikah.special_notes: Sikah is unique - tonic on E half-flat, often explores below tonic

nikriz.special_notes: The augmented second (3-4) is the signature sound - must be highlighted

hijazkar.special_notes: Double Hijaz - both lower and upper tetrachords have augmented second

ajam_ushayran.special_notes: Ajam starting from Ushayran (G), ascending character

bayati_shuri.special_notes: Bayati with Hijaz upper jins - dramatic raised 7th degree

farahfaza.special_notes: Nahawand with Hijaz upper - dramatic augmented second in upper register

nawa_athar.special_notes: Double augmented seconds - Nikriz lower with Hijaz upper, very dramatic

suznak.special_notes: Rast with Hijaz upper - combines the balanced Rast lower with dramatic upper

huzam.special_notes: Sikah with Hijaz upper - mystical base with exotic augmented second

saba_zamzam.special_notes: Darker variant of Saba with lowered 2nd - more intense mourning quality

jiharkah.special_notes: Lydian-like raised 4th degree - bright, elevated character

lami.special_notes: Rare maqam with unique quarter-tone on 4th degree - ancient, unusual character

sikah_baladi.special_notes: Egyptian folk variant of Sikah - wider intervals, popular character

iraq.special_notes: Symmetrical quarter-tone maqam - mystical, floating quality

yakah.special_notes: Rast starting from Yakah (G below) - ascending character, fundamental

generic_sayr_template.description: Template for maqamat without specific sayr definitions
